## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `ui/detail/panel_registry.dart:1` (631 lines) — contains five distinct concerns: **RESOLVED:** concerns 4 and 5 moved to `detail_id_dispatch.dart` (Step-8 builders + DetailIdDispatch, 166 ln) and `ai_panel_dispatch.dart` (Step-9 builders + AiPanelDispatch/AiPanelSelection, 229 ln) as part files — external consumers keep importing `panel_registry.dart` (now 241 ln, target met) and the single mirror test stays valid. All 320 feature tests pass.
  1. `SettingsPanelSpec` / `kSettingsPanels` registry constant (lines 63–168)
  2. `panelSpecFor` resolver and `kSettingsPanelSwapDuration` constant (lines 172–184)
  3. Step-7 simple-leaf builder functions (lines 186–239)
  4. `DetailIdDispatch` widget + Step-8 dynamic-list builders (lines 241–403)
  5. `AiPanelDispatch` / `AiPanelSelection` / `aiPanelSelectionFor` + Step-9 AI + agents builders (lines 404–631)

  Concrete split: extract `detail_id_dispatch.dart` (concern 4) and `ai_panel_dispatch.dart` (concern 5). Each is an independently testable widget; `panel_registry.dart` would drop to ~200 lines holding only the registry constant and spec type. The corresponding test at 1 252 lines would naturally mirror-split to three files.

- [x] **[MED]** `panel_registry_test.dart:1` (1 252 lines) — when `panel_registry.dart` is split, move `DetailIdDispatch` tests to `detail_id_dispatch_test.dart` and `AiPanelDispatch` / `aiPanelSelectionFor` tests to `ai_panel_dispatch_test.dart`. The "registered ids" and "scrollable flag" groups stay in `panel_registry_test.dart`.
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change — `detail_id_dispatch.dart` and `ai_panel_dispatch.dart` are `part of panel_registry.dart` (one library), so the one-test-file-per-source rule keeps a single mirror; a split was attempted and fails outright (`part of` files cannot be imported). The move applies only if the parts ever become standalone libraries.

- [x] **[MED]** `ui/widgets/settings_v2_top_crumbs.dart` (184 lines) — test at 422 lines; ratio is 2.3×. Currently solid but worth monitoring; if the crumbs widget grows, extract a `_CrumbsTestBench` to avoid duplicating setup.
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change — the item itself rates the file solid and asks only for monitoring; ratio unchanged.

- [x] **[LOW]** `ui/widgets/settings_tree_resize_handle_test.dart` (424 lines) vs impl (156 lines) — the test coverage is excellent but the 2.7× ratio signals that helper extraction (`_drag`, `_drainTimers`, `_pumpHandle`) is already doing good work. Keep as-is.
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change — the item explicitly says "keep as-is". The shared `_drag` / `_drainTimers` / `_pumpHandle` helpers already absorb the boilerplate (drag warmup, debounce-timer draining, pump), so the high line count is meaningful coverage, not duplication. No further DRY win available.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[MED]** `panel_registry_test.dart:535` — the `setUp` in "panel registry — list/create/detail closures" manually checks `getIt.isRegistered<JournalDb>()` and conditionally registers, violating the "use `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()`" rule. If `getIt` already has a `JournalDb` registered from a previous test in the suite, this guard silently reuses it, creating cross-test contamination risk. Replace with `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt()` per-test or use `addTearDown(getIt.reset)` unconditionally.
  **RESOLVED:** done — the conditional `isRegistered` guard is gone; the group now uses `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt` so the closures always see fresh central mocks instead of silently reusing leftovers.

  ```dart
  // Current (fragile):
  setUp(() {
    if (!getIt.isRegistered<JournalDb>()) {
      getIt.registerSingleton<JournalDb>(MockJournalDb());
    }
    ...
  });

  // Fix:
  setUp(() async {
    await setUpTestGetIt();
  });
  tearDown(() async => tearDownTestGetIt());
  ```

- [x] **[MED]** `panel_registry_test.dart:176–299` (SettingsPanelSpec — builders group) — `build('sync-node-profile')` returns a `SyncNodeProfilePage` (a full `Scaffold`-owning page) and `build('sync')` returns a `Consumer`. These two builders are not asserted in the "every registered builder returns the expected Body widget" `testWidgets`. Only step-7 through step-9 bodies are checked; `sync-node-profile` and the `sync` consumer go un-type-checked. Add:
  ```dart
  expect(build('sync-node-profile'), isA<SyncNodeProfilePage>());
  expect(build('sync'), isA<Consumer>());
  ```
  **RESOLVED:** done — added the two missing assertions: `build('sync-node-profile')` is a `SyncNodeProfilePage` and `build('sync')` is a `Consumer`; every registered builder is now type-checked.

- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_tree_scope_test.dart` — uses `pumpAndSettle` once (line ~190) after a route-change notification that triggers only a `setState` rebuild. A `pump()` is sufficient and deterministic.
  **RESOLVED:** done (corrected) — the `pumpAndSettle` actually settles the host's `configFlagProvider` Stream emission (not a `setState`/route-change as the item described). Replaced with two bounded `pump()` calls: one to deliver the mock's `watchConfigFlag` `false` emission, one to apply the provider rebuild. The tree assertions hold either way because the host falls back to `false` via `.value ?? false`; the bounded pumps drain the stream deterministically with no settle-loop.

- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_tree_node_widget_test.dart` — 2 `pumpAndSettle` calls; both follow `pump()` sequences that already drain async work. Replacing with `pump()` is safe.
  **RESOLVED:** done — replaced both `pumpAndSettle(400ms)` calls with `pump()` (apply the open state) + `pump(const Duration(milliseconds: 400))` (advance past every branch animation in `SettingsV2Constants`, max 260 ms). The assertions (path value, `Align.heightFactor` target) never required animation completion, but the bounded second pump settles the implicit `AnimatedSize`/`AnimatedOpacity` tickers so they don't leak — a single zero-duration `pump()` would leave a running ticker and trip the post-test pending-timer check.

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

The `settings_tree_index_test.dart` already carries two well-crafted Glados tests:
1. **`beamUrlToPath` round-trip** (url → path → url) across all registered nodes and generated suffixes (180 runs).
2. **`SettingsTreeIndex` flag-gated ancestor invariants** across all flag combinations (80 runs).

**No additional Glados tests are warranted for this feature.** The pure functions are already covered:

- `pathToBeamUrl` / `beamUrlToPath` / `_canonicalize` / `_idToPath` — all covered by the existing Glados suite.
- `buildSettingsTree` — takes flag booleans → `List<SettingsNode>`; the invariants (stable root order, flag-gated presence) are already covered by the Glados "ancestor invariants" property that rebuilds the tree for every flag combination.
- `aiPanelSelectionFor` — pure function with 4 branches (provider, model, profile, list); fully covered by unit tests; not a Glados fit (finite enum-like dispatch).
- `filterDisplayedFlags` — lives in `settings`, not `settings_v2`; flagged in the settings review.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[MED]** `panel_registry_test.dart` — the `AiPanelDispatch` widget group (lines 700+) is well-tested for `aiPanelSelectionFor`, but the `AiPanelDispatch` widget's **`StackFit.expand` layoutBuilder** branch is only indirectly asserted (via the cross-fade test). A test that verifies the `AnimatedSwitcher`'s `layoutBuilder` produces a `Stack` with `StackFit.expand` would pin the regression-prone layout policy. This matters because a `StackFit.loose` regression silently collapses Scaffold-based children.
  **RESOLVED:** done — added a test invoking the `AnimatedSwitcher.layoutBuilder` directly and asserting it produces a `Stack` with `StackFit.expand` (a `StackFit.loose` regression would silently collapse Scaffold-based children).
- [x] **[MED]** `ui/detail/leaf_panel.dart` — the `scrollable: true` / `scrollable: false` dispatch wraps (or does not wrap) the panel body in a `SingleChildScrollView`. The test at 417 lines exercises both paths, but the "unbounded-height panic" scenario (a `ListView` inside a `scrollable: true` panel) is commented-out in the registry comment but not asserted. A test that passes a `ListView`-returning builder with `scrollable: false` and verifies no overflow would catch future misclassification.
  **RESOLVED:** done (adapted) — `kSettingsPanels` is const, so a synthetic ListView spec can't be injected through LeafPanel; the failure mode is pinned as an executable spec instead: the same list-owning body renders cleanly unwrapped (scrollable:false path) and throws with unbounded height inside the scrollable wrap, with the per-panel flag values pinned in panel_registry_test.
- [x] **[LOW]** `ui/url_sync/settings_tree_url_sync.dart` — the `SchedulerBinding.instance.scheduleTask` path for the initial-load URL-to-path sync on first frame is exercised in the test via `tester.pump()`, but the "initial route is a deep-link that includes a panel-local UUID" scenario is not tested (e.g. `/settings/categories/abc-123` on first load should collapse to the `categories` tree node).
  **RESOLVED:** done — added a `URL → tree` test that mounts with `initialRoute: /settings/categories/abc-123` (UUID present on the *first* frame, not appended later). It asserts the post-frame seed greedily collapses to `['definitions', 'definitions/categories']` and that `spy.uris` stays empty, pinning the `_urlDrivenSyncs` guard that prevents the seed from beaming the canonical `/settings/categories` and stripping the `/abc-123` segment before the panel dispatcher reads it. The pre-existing seed test omitted the no-beam assertion; this strengthens it for the mount-time-UUID case.
- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_tree_controller_test.dart` — the `isValidPath`-guard behavior when a stale URL (from a previous session with Matrix enabled) is installed after Matrix is disabled is not tested end-to-end. The lower-level `SettingsTreeIndex.isValidPath` test covers the pure logic; the controller test does not verify the controller falls back to `[]` when the persisted path becomes invalid after a flag change.
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change — the described controller behavior does not exist. `isValidPath` is defined only in `settings_tree_index.dart` and is referenced from **nowhere** in `lib/` (verified by grep); `SettingsTreePath` has no path persistence and no flag-change fallback. `syncFromUrl` resolves via the flag-independent static `settingsNodeUrls` map (`beamUrlToPath`), so a "stale URL after a flag change" still resolves to its path and there is no controller path that consults `isValidPath` to fall back to `[]`. The pure logic is already covered in `settings_tree_index_test.dart` (lines 505–541 + the flag-gated invariants at 406–431). Writing a controller test for non-existent behavior would fabricate coverage.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [x] **[MED]** `panel_registry_test.dart` — the "every registered builder returns the expected Body widget" `testWidgets` (lines 176–299) pumps a `MaterialApp` and captures a `BuildContext` to invoke 28 builders. This is a single widget test body that is ~120 lines long. Because none of the builder results are pumped (only type-checked), this could be a plain `test()` with a synthetic `BuildContext` stub. Moving it out of `testWidgets` would eliminate the Flutter test-binding overhead for a non-widget test. **Estimated impact: minor per-test (< 1 s) but avoids a 120-line critical-path widget test.**
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change — the builders receive the captured `BuildContext` and several constructed pages may legitimately read it; replacing the pumped context with a synthetic stub trades a <1 s saving for a brittle fake that breaks the moment any constructor touches the context. The single pump stays.
- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_v2_top_crumbs_test.dart` — 2 `pumpAndSettle` calls; both follow `setState`-style mutations where `pump()` is sufficient.
  **RESOLVED:** done — replaced both `pumpAndSettle()` calls (after tapping a crumb link) with a single bounded `pump()`. The crumb tap calls `truncateTo` synchronously, so one `pump()` applies the rebuild; the InkWell splash that `pumpAndSettle` was draining is irrelevant to the path-value assertion and its controller is disposed at teardown. This mirrors the established tap-then-`pump()` pattern already used in `settings_tree_node_widget_test.dart` ("tapping a leaf selects it").
- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_tree_node_widget_test.dart` — 2 `pumpAndSettle` calls; same as above.
  **RESOLVED:** done — see the [LOW] entry above under "Test quality improvements"; both `pumpAndSettle(400ms)` calls were converted to `pump()` + bounded `pump(400ms)`.
- [x] **[LOW]** `settings_tree_scope_test.dart` — 1 `pumpAndSettle` after a `ValueNotifier` update; `pump()` is enough.
  **RESOLVED:** done (corrected) — see the [LOW] entry above under "Test quality improvements". The trigger is a `configFlagProvider` Stream emission, not a `ValueNotifier` update; replaced `pumpAndSettle()` with two bounded `pump()` calls (deliver emission + apply rebuild).
- [x] **[LOW]** `panel_registry_test.dart:533–557` — the `setUp` for the list/detail/create group pumps a `MaterialApp` twice (once inside `dispatchFor`, once in the builder test above). The `dispatchFor` helper already encapsulates the pump; a shared `setUpAll` that calls `setUpTestGetIt()` once would remove per-test warm-up. **Estimated impact: ~10–15 % speed-up for that group.**
  **RESOLVED:** assessed, no change (unsafe) — this directly conflicts with the already-applied MED correctness fix above (`panel_registry_test.dart:535`), which deliberately moved this group to per-test `setUpTestGetIt()` / `tearDownTestGetIt` to eliminate cross-test contamination. `setUpTestGetIt()` calls `getIt.reset()` and is paired with a per-test `tearDownTestGetIt`; hoisting it into `setUpAll` while keeping the per-test `tearDown` would unregister singletons after the first test and break the rest, and sharing one reset across the group reintroduces exactly the contamination the MED item fixed. The `dispatchFor` `MaterialApp` pump is per-test by necessity — each test needs a live `BuildContext` to invoke the builder closures. The <1 s saving is not worth violating the central getIt-helper contract.

---

## Summary

- **1 oversized impl** file: `panel_registry.dart` (631 lines) with three natural split seams (registry, `DetailIdDispatch`, `AiPanelDispatch`+helpers).
- **1 oversized test** file: `panel_registry_test.dart` (1 252 lines) — would split cleanly when the impl splits.
- **2 weak-test issues**: manual `getIt.isRegistered` guard in `panel_registry_test` setUp (contamination risk); missing type-assertions for `sync-node-profile` and `sync` builders in the "every builder" test.
- **0 Glados candidates** — the index's URL ↔ path and ancestor-invariant properties are already covered by two high-quality Glados tests; no new generative tests are needed in this feature.
- **4 speed wins** (all LOW/MED): replace 5 unnecessary `pumpAndSettle` calls with `pump()` across the tree-widget tests; convert the "every builder" testWidgets into a plain `test()`.
- **Biggest opportunity**: split `panel_registry.dart` into three files — it is currently doing the work of a registry, a dispatch widget, and an AI-specific dispatch widget in one place. The split has no correctness risk (all behavior is already fully tested) and will make future panel additions smaller and easier to review.
